req.title: Device data ingest
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test dimensions: (1) a registered device successfully pushing FHIR Observations with normalization applied (positive/EP), (2) an unregistered or cert-invalid device being rejected with HTTP 401 (negative/NEG), (3) a boundary condition where exactly one Observation is submitted — the minimum non-empty payload — ensuring the ingest pipeline handles singular resources correctly (boundary/BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'device registered' to 'observation stored' ensuring idempotent re-submission does not duplicate records (STC). A fifth test uses a decision table to validate that a missing required FHIR field causes rejection while a complete payload succeeds (DT).

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_stores_observation_with_normalization
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Valid device TLS certificate is available
actions: POST /Ingest with a valid FHIR Observation bundle using a registered device cert
Assert stored Observation is retrievable via GET /Observation/{id}
Assert normalization fields (e.g. unit canonicalization) are present on the stored resource
trace: R-HLT-022: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"

@pytest.fixture
def registered_device_session():
    return <fixture_registered_device_session>

@pytest.fixture
def fhir_observation_payload():
    return <fixture_fhir_observation_payload>

def test_registered_device_ingest_stores_observation_with_normalization(
    registered_device_session, fhir_observation_payload
):
    resp = registered_device_session.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest",
        json=fhir_observation_payload,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Observation"
    obs_id = body.get("id")
    assert obs_id, "Observation id must not be null"
    get_resp = registered_device_session.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Observation/{obs_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    stored = get_resp.json()
    unit = stored.get("valueQuantity", {}).get("unit")
    assert unit == <EXPECTED_NORMALIZED_UNIT>, f"Normalization failed: unit={unit}"
    <helper_cleanup_observation>(obs_id, registered_device_session)

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_ingest_returns_401
preconditions: An invalid or unregistered device TLS certificate is available
actions: POST /Ingest with a valid FHIR Observation bundle using an unregistered/invalid device cert
expected: Response body contains error detail referencing invalid certificate or device not found
No new Observation resource created in the system
trace: R-HLT-022: unregistered devices shall be rejected

tests.name: test_ingest_single_observation_boundary_payload
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Valid device TLS certificate is available
actions: Construct a FHIR Bundle containing exactly one Observation entry (minimum non-empty bundle)
Assert exactly one Observation is stored
trace: BVA: minimum bundle size of 1 Observation

tests.name: test_idempotent_resubmission_does_not_duplicate_observation
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Valid device TLS certificate is available
A specific Observation with a known identifier has not yet been ingested
actions: POST /Ingest with a FHIR Observation that carries a unique identifier
POST /Ingest with the identical payload a second time
expected: Exactly one Observation with the given identifier exists after both submissions
trace: STC: device transitions from 'pending ingest' to 'ingested'; re-trigger must not create duplicate state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"
OBS_IDENTIFIER = "urn:test:idempotent-obs-001"

@pytest.fixture
def registered_device_session():
    return <fixture_registered_device_session>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup(registered_device_session):
    <helper_delete_observations_by_identifier>(OBS_IDENTIFIER, registered_device_session)
    yield
    <helper_delete_observations_by_identifier>(OBS_IDENTIFIER, registered_device_session)

def test_idempotent_resubmission_does_not_duplicate_observation(registered_device_session):
    payload = <helper_build_fhir_observation>(identifier=OBS_IDENTIFIER)
    r1 = registered_device_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest", json=payload)
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 201), f"First POST failed: {r1.status_code}"
    r2 = registered_device_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest", json=payload)
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 201), f"Second POST failed: {r2.status_code}"
    search = registered_device_session.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Observation",
        params={"identifier": OBS_IDENTIFIER},
    )
    assert search.status_code == 200
    total = search.json().get("total", -1)
    assert total == 1, f"Expected 1 Observation, found {total}"

tests.name: test_decision_table_missing_required_fhir_field_rejected
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Valid device TLS certificate is available
actions: Case A: POST /Ingest with complete, valid FHIR Observation — expect 201
Case B: POST /Ingest with Observation missing required 'status' field — expect 422
Case C: POST /Ingest with Observation missing required 'subject' field — expect 422
trace: R-HLT-022: unregistered/malformed devices shall be rejected
DT: combinations of required field presence/absence
tests.executable:
import pytest
import copy
import requests

BASE_URL = "<BASE_URL>"

@pytest.fixture
def registered_device_session():
    return <fixture_registered_device_session>

@pytest.fixture
def base_obs():
    return <fixture_fhir_observation_payload>()

def test_decision_table_missing_required_fhir_field_rejected(
    registered_device_session, base_obs
):
    # Case A: complete payload
    r_ok = registered_device_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest", json=base_obs)
    assert r_ok.status_code == 201, f"Case A failed: {r_ok.status_code}"
    obs_id = r_ok.json().get("id")
    <helper_cleanup_observation>(obs_id, registered_device_session)

    # Case B: missing 'status'
    no_status = copy.deepcopy(base_obs)
    no_status.pop("status", None)
    r_b = registered_device_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest", json=no_status)
    assert r_b.status_code == 422, f"Case B expected 422, got {r_b.status_code}"
    assert r_b.json().get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

    # Case C: missing 'subject'
    no_subject = copy.deepcopy(base_obs)
    no_subject.pop("subject", None)
    r_c = registered_device_session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Ingest", json=no_subject)
    assert r_c.status_code == 422, f"Case C expected 422, got {r_c.status_code}"
    assert r_c.json().get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
